header.prompt:
You are building the complete visual language for a new rail-shooter level in the repo at /tmp/raild-run-eztdwig5twea (work in that directory).

FIRST read these, in order:
1. /tmp/mass-driver-design.md — the level design document and cross-module contract. It is binding, especially the Palette / Enemies / Visuals sections.
2. /tmp/raild-run-eztdwig5twea/AGENTS.md (gotchas: HDR only on thin lines/small cores; setFromAxisAngle axes must be unit length; WebGPU-only) and docs/level-authoring.md (visual factories, glyphs, post-processing).
3. src/engine/visual-kit.ts, src/engine/environment-kit.ts, src/engine/camera-feel.ts, src/engine/rail.ts, src/engine/glyphs.ts, src/engine/locks.ts, src/engine/types.ts (VisualFactories)
4. The Helios visuals as the quality bar and structural template — src/levels/helios/visuals/index.ts, environment.ts, enemies.ts, effects.ts, letters.ts, palette.ts, post-fx.ts (read palette.ts and post-fx.ts too). Mirror the architecture (records via createPendingVisualRecords, adornment slots, transient effect pools, TintPart parts arrays with distance-falloff tinting, event choreography). Never import from another level's directory.
5. src/levels/mass-driver-wo4m/timing.ts and src/levels/mass-driver-wo4m/index.ts (both already written — index.ts consumes your exports; do NOT edit either).

YOUR FILES (you own the visuals/ directory exclusively): replace src/levels/mass-driver-wo4m/visuals/index.ts and create palette.ts, environment.ts, enemies.ts, effects.ts, letters.ts, post-fx.ts under src/levels/mass-driver-wo4m/visuals/.
Do not create or edit files outside visuals/. Another agent is concurrently writing gameplay.ts — it may not exist in final form when you start. Its exports are FIXED by the design doc contract (createMassDriverRail, massDriverRunProgress, speedFactorAt, MUZZLE_U, INTERLOCK_COUNT, kinds 'coil'|'threader'|'capacitor'|'arc'|'interlock'). Import them from '../gameplay' per the contract and build your leaf files first (palette → letters → enemies → effects → post-fx), environment.ts and index.ts last, so gameplay.ts has likely landed by then. A third agent owns audio.ts — ignore errors there.

Build, per the design doc (key points, the doc has the full spec):
- palette.ts: VOID background, GUNMETAL structure, ARC_BLUE / VOLT_VIOLET / ION_WHITE, heatColor(t) ramp blue→violet→white, HAZARD amber (reserved for interlocks/warnings/denial/detonation), LOCK_GRADIENT (arc blue → violet → white), hdr() helper like Helios.
- environment.ts: the accelerator-ring tunnel. 112 beat rings at u = massDriverRunProgress(ringBeatTime(b)), oriented to the rail frame (sampleRailFrame basis), colored by heatColor(b/111), downbeat rings heavier with coil-housing lugs; use InstancedMesh (rings + lugs) so check:perf passes. Track the camera's beat crossings each frame and expose a per-frame update hook that: flashes the just-passed ring (an overlay pulse mesh + instance color boost that decays), pre-glows the next ring, and during bars 20–28 strobes all rings ahead hotter toward white (charge). 4 HDR conductor-rail tubes along the barrel at ±45° (TubeGeometry over offset rail samples, ending at MUZZLE_U), dark faceted wall panels/ribs via scatterAlongRail at radius ~12–13 with sparse dim service lights, FogExp2 (~5–6 rings visible) with createAtmosphereRamp keyed on run progress (fog/background warm from blue-black toward violet, whiten through the interlock bars, then cut to near-zero density + pure black space after MUZZLE_U), starfield points + thin star-streak sprites + one distant beacon for the muzzle section, camera-attached speed streaks whose scroll rate follows speedFactorAt (slam bright after the shot), and a growing white-hot charge glow down the bore during interlock bars. Attract mode: breech idling, rings dim, slow pulse.
- enemies.ts: distinct silhouettes per the doc — coil (hex pod, blue edge glow), threader (long needle + fins + tail streak), capacitor (insulated cylinder, armor staves that visibly break off on 'stage', crackling core), arc (ball-lightning: bright core + jittering jagged shells; mark isHostileShot + trailColor), interlock (heavy hazard-striped X-clamp; armor cowl pops at stage 1 exposing a hot actuator core). All meshes get userData.parts TintPart arrays (edge/fill/core) with base colors so index.ts's tint pass handles lock (ion white)/denied (hazard red)/damage-flash, Helios-style. Keep every enemy readable at gameplay distance with bloom OFF.
- effects.ts: transient pools — electric spark bursts (jagged shard lines), lock hex-clamp rings, ring pulses, glints, brief jagged arc-lightning polylines between two points (kills, capacitor crackle), muzzle whiteout burst, hazard-red detonation burst, projectile trails. resetEffects/updateEffects like Helios.
- letters.ts: CHARGE/RELOAD glyphs (C,H,A,R,G,E,L,O,D) from engine glyphOnCells, styled as stencil plates on gun housing: dark plate cells + electric-blue HDR edges; locked → ion-white; denied → hazard red. Legible at distance 20, bloom off.
- post-fx.ts: module-scope uniforms (flashUniform, chargeUniform, plus a hazard tint path for detonation) and export composeMassDriverOutput following Helios's TSL pattern (additive over base; engine owns vignette).
- index.ts: mirror Helios's visuals/index.ts — pending records, factories, reticle (breech charge-gauge: thin outer ring + SIX arc segments that light per lock + center dot, counter-rotation when active, colorForLockCount with your LOCK_GRADIENT), full event choreography for spawn/lock/unlock/fire/hit/stage/kill/miss/reject(denied)/volley/beat/playerhit/bossphase/runstart/runend per the design doc (bossphase destroyed → full-tunnel white strobe; SHOT_TIME crossing → whiteout+FOV kick+shake+streak slam; detonation keyed off playerhit damage ≥90 or runend died), updateVisuals + updateCameraEffects (FOV breathes with speedFactorAt, subtle downbeat kick, bank into rail weave; camera-feel shake constants tuned for a metallic gun-barrel rattle).

VERIFY before finishing:
- `npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json 2>&1 | grep "levels/mass-driver-wo4m/visuals"` → must be empty (errors in gameplay.ts/audio.ts are other agents').
- Snapshot your meshes and LOOK at the PNGs: `npm run snapshot -- --module src/levels/mass-driver-wo4m/visuals/enemies.ts --export <eachFactory>` for every enemy factory and at least one letter; iterate silhouettes until they read. (Snapshots render via a WebGL fallback; that's expected for this tool.)
- If gameplay.ts exists by the time you finish: `npm run snapshot:gameplay -- --level mass-driver-wo4m --thumbnails 8` and inspect the sheet for tunnel readability, ring rhythm, bloom discipline (no whiteout), and the post-shot starfield. Iterate.
- Watch perf: `npm run check:perf -- --level mass-driver-wo4m` if gameplay exists; instancing and pooled effects should keep it green.

Return (as your final message): what you built per file, snapshot findings and what you changed because of them, perf/typecheck status, and anything the integrator must eyeball in a real playtest.
header.finalResultText:
Everything I own is complete and verified. The `check:scope` exit=1 is a pre-existing property of the benchmark branch (it diffs against `main`, which lists every other level and the benchmark infra already committed on this run branch) — `git status` confirms my only changes are inside `src/levels/mass-driver-wo4m/visuals/`. The modified `src/levels/index.ts` (registry line) was written by another agent and is an allowed scope path anyway.

## Summary

I built the complete visual language for the Mass Driver level across the seven files in `src/levels/mass-driver-wo4m/visuals/`, mirroring the Helios architecture (pending records, adornment slots, transient pools, `TintPart` distance-falloff tinting, event choreography).

**What I built, per file:**

- **`palette.ts`** — `VOID` blue-black, `GUNMETAL` structure, `ARC_BLUE`/`VOLT_VIOLET`/`ION_WHITE`, `heatColor(t)` two-segment ramp (blue→violet→white), reserved `HAZARD`/`HAZARD_RED`, `LOCK_GRADIENT` (arc blue→violet→blinding), `hdr()`.
- **`letters.ts`** — C,H,A,R,G,E,L,O,D from `glyphOnCells` as stencil plates: dark gunmetal cells with electric-blue HDR routed edges; locked→ion white, denied→hazard red.
- **`enemies.ts`** — five distinct silhouettes: coil (hex wall pod + clamp hooks + eye), threader (long needle + fins + violet tail streak), capacitor (insulated bank with 6 shearable staves + crackling core, `breakCapacitorArmor`), arc (ball-lightning core in jittering jagged shells, `isHostileShot`+`trailColor`), interlock (hazard-striped X-clamp with poppable cowl over a hot actuator, `breakInterlockCowl`). All expose `userData.parts` for the tint pass.
- **`effects.ts`** — pooled electric spark shards (instanced), ring pulses, glints, jagged arc-lightning polylines, camera-facing flash disc (muzzle whiteout / detonation core), projectile trails; `resetEffects`/`updateEffects`.
- **`post-fx.ts`** — module-scope `flashUniform`, `chargeUniform` (radial white-violet bore glow), `detonationUniform` (hazard red/white), composited additively in `composeMassDriverOutput`.
- **`environment.ts`** — 112 InstancedMesh accelerator rings on `massDriverRunProgress(ringBeatTime(b))` oriented to the rail frame, heat-colored, downbeats heavier with lugs; per-frame beat-crossing detection that flashes the passed ring, pre-glows the next, and strobes rings-ahead toward white through the charge bars; 4 HDR conductor-rail tubes; scattered barrel-wall rib panels with service lights; `FogExp2` + `createAtmosphereRamp` (blue→violet→white, cutting to black vacuum past `MUZZLE_U`); camera-attached speed streaks scaled by `speedFactorAt`; post-muzzle starfield + star-streaks + beacon; a growing white-hot charge glow down the bore; `strobeRings()` and attract-mode dimming.
- **`index.ts`** — six-segment breech charge-gauge reticle (lights one per lock via `LOCK_GRADIENT`, counter-rotates when active), full event choreography (spawn/lock/unlock/fire/hit/stage/kill/miss/reject/volley/beat/bossphase/playerhit/runstart/runend), SHOT_TIME whiteout + FOV kick + metallic-rattle shake + muzzle flash, detonation keyed off `playerhit` damage≥90 / `runend died`, `updateVisuals`/`updateCameraEffects` (FOV breathes with speed, downbeat kick, rail-weave bank).

**Snapshot findings / changes made:** All five enemy factories and the letter render legibly with the silhouettes intended (verified PNGs). I lowered the interlock actuator-core intensity (2.0→1.35) so the cowl-pop reveal reads as a real reveal rather than a pre-lit core. The gameplay contact sheet confirmed ring rhythm, blue→violet heat progression, wall/enemy readability, the charge whitening ramp, the full whiteout at the shot (t≈52.5s), and the star-streaked black-space muzzle exit.

**Perf/typecheck:** `npx tsc` — 0 errors, visuals grep empty. `npm run build` passes. `npm run check:perf` — all gates green after I fixed a geometry leak (the runner removes enemy/projectile meshes but doesn't free geometry; I made both `disposeRecord`s dispose the mesh — geometry count late dropped from 892→95, and to 26 post-shot).

**For the integrator to eyeball in a real WebGPU playtest:**
- The charge crescendo (bars ~24–28) is intentionally very bright and the shot is a ~0.6s full whiteout — confirm it reads as a launch and builds tension without fatigue at the player's bloom setting.
- The six-segment reticle charge-gauge legibility during live play (subtle at distance by design).
- The metallic gun-barrel camera rattle feel (tight, roll-biased, 12 Hz).
- Arc ball-lightning shell jitter uses per-frame `Math.random` (non-deterministic, visuals-only) — expected, but note it won't be identical between seeded snapshot runs.

events.resultText:
1	# MASS DRIVER — design document and cross-module contract
2	
3	Level id `mass-driver-wo4m`, title "Mass Driver", directory `src/levels/mass-driver-wo4m/`.
4	Repo: /tmp/raild-run-eztdwig5twea. Read `AGENTS.md`, `docs/level-brief.md`, `docs/level-authoring.md` first.
5	The quality bar is `src/levels/helios/` — read its spine for calibration (gameplay.ts, audio.ts, visuals/index.ts and the leaf you're mirroring). **Never import from another level's directory** — mirror patterns, don't import. Only touch files inside `src/levels/mass-driver-wo4m/` that are assigned to you.
6	
7	## Fantasy
8	
9	You are riding a payload down a huge orbital railgun. The barrel is a tunnel of glowing
10	accelerator rings and you pass through **one ring exactly on every beat** — speed and music are
11	the same thing. The run accelerates the whole way: rings space out in world units but always land
12	on the beat. "Hot" is electric, not fire: arc blue → violet → blinding white. The gun itself is
13	the instrument — a bass hum that climbs in pitch across the entire run, under a locked, hypnotic
14	128 BPM pulse. Enemies are defense drones threading between the coils. Finale: the gun's safety
15	interlocks are jammed and the firing charge is already building; destroy all six interlocks before
16	the charge peaks at bar 28 and the gun fires, launching you out of the muzzle into silent open
17	space at insane speed. Fail and the barrel detonates with you inside it.
18	
19	## Shared timing (ALREADY WRITTEN — import, don't redefine)
20	
21	`src/levels/mass-driver-wo4m/timing.ts` exports:
22	- `MASS_DRIVER_BPM` (128), `MASS_DRIVER_STEPS_PER_BAR` (16), `MASS_DRIVER_TIME`, `MASS_DRIVER_BAR`, `BEAT_SECONDS`
23	- `MASS_DRIVER_BARS = { injection: 0, stage1: 4, stage2: 12, interlock: 20, shot: 28, end: 32 }`
24	- `MASS_DRIVER_MARKERS`, `MASS_DRIVER_DURATION` (= 60.0s exactly), `STAGE1_TIME`, `STAGE2_TIME`, `INTERLOCK_TIME`, `SHOT_TIME`
25	- `RING_BEATS` (112), `ringBeatTime(beat)` (= beat × BEAT_SECONDS

events.text: Let me quickly understand the scope check to report accurately, since git shows I only touched files in visuals/.

events.resultText:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

usage() {
  echo "Usage: npm run check:scope -- <level-id> [base-ref]" >&2
}

if [ "$#" -lt 1 ] || [ "$#" -gt 2 ]; then
  usage
  exit 2
fi

level_id=$1
base_ref=${2:-main}
allowed_prefix="src/levels/${level_id}/"
allowed_registry="src/levels/index.ts"
allowed_gallery="docs/level-gallery.md"

if ! git rev-parse --verify --quiet "$base_ref" >/dev/null; then
  echo "Unknown base ref: $base_ref" >&2
  exit 2
fi

changed_files=$(
  {
    git diff --name-only "$base_ref"
    git ls-files --others --exclude-standard
  } | sort -u
)

[ -n "$changed_files" ] || exit 0

out_of_scope=$(printf '%s\n' "$changed_files" | awk -v prefix="$allowed_prefix" -v registry="$allowed_registry" -v gallery="$allowed_gallery" '
  $0 != registry && $0 != gallery && index($0, prefix) != 1 { print }
')

if [ -n "$out_of_scope" ]; then
  echo "Out-of-scope files for level '$level_id':" >&2
  printf '%s\n' "$out_of_scope" >&2
  exit 1
fi

events.text:
Everything I own is complete and verified. The `check:scope` exit=1 is a pre-existing property of the benchmark branch (it diffs against `main`, which lists every other level and the benchmark infra already committed on this run branch) — `git status` confirms my only changes are inside `src/levels/mass-driver-wo4m/visuals/`. The modified `src/levels/index.ts` (registry line) was written by another agent and is an allowed scope path anyway.

## Summary

I built the complete visual language for the Mass Driver level across the seven files in `src/levels/mass-driver-wo4m/visuals/`, mirroring the Helios architecture (pending records, adornment slots, transient pools, `TintPart` distance-falloff tinting, event choreography).

**What I built, per file:**

- **`palette.ts`** — `VOID` blue-black, `GUNMETAL` structure, `ARC_BLUE`/`VOLT_VIOLET`/`ION_WHITE`, `heatColor(t)` two-segment ramp (blue→violet→white), reserved `HAZARD`/`HAZARD_RED`, `LOCK_GRADIENT` (arc blue→violet→blinding), `hdr()`.
- **`letters.ts`** — C,H,A,R,G,E,L,O,D from `glyphOnCells` as stencil plates: dark gunmetal cells with electric-blue HDR routed edges; locked→ion white, denied→hazard red.
- **`enemies.ts`** — five distinct silhouettes: coil (hex wall pod + clamp hooks + eye), threader (long needle + fins + violet tail streak), capacitor (insulated bank with 6 shearable staves + crackling core, `breakCapacitorArmor`), arc (ball-lightning core in jittering jagged shells, `isHostileShot`+`trailColor`), interlock (hazard-striped X-clamp with poppable cowl over a hot actuator, `breakInterlockCowl`). All expose `userData.parts` for the tint pass.
- **`effects.ts`** — pooled electric spark shards (instanced), ring pulses, glints, jagged arc-lightning polylines, camera-facing flash disc (muzzle whiteout / detonation core), projectile trails; `resetEffects`/`updateEffects`.
- **`post-fx.ts`** — module-scope `flashUniform`, `chargeUniform` (radial white-violet bore glow), `detonationUniform` (hazard red/white), composited additively in `composeMassDriverOutput`.
- **`environment.ts`** — 112 InstancedMesh accelerator rings on `massDriverRunProgress(ringBeatTime(b))` oriented to the rail frame, heat-colored, downbeats heavier with lugs; per-frame beat-crossing detection that flashes the passed ring, pre-glows the next, and strobes rings-ahead toward white through the charge bars; 4 HDR conductor-rail tubes; scattered barrel-wall rib panels with service lights; `FogExp2` + `createAtmosphereRamp` (blue→violet→white, cutting to black vacuum past `MUZZLE_U`); camera-attached speed streaks scaled by `speedFactorAt`; post-muzzle starfield + star-streaks + beacon; a growing white-hot charge glow down the bore; `strobeRings()` and attract-mode dimming.
- **`index.ts`** — six-segment breech charge-gauge reticle (lights one per lock via `LOCK_GRADIENT`, counter-rotates when active), full event choreography (spawn/lock/unlock/fire/hit/stage/kill/miss/reject/volley/beat/bossphase/playerhit/runstart/runend), SHOT_TIME whiteout + FOV kick + metallic-rattle shake + muzzle flash, detonation keyed off `playerhit` damage≥90 / `runend died`, `updateVisuals`/`updateCameraEffects` (FOV breathes with speed, downbeat kick, rail-weave bank).

**Snapshot findings / changes made:** All five enemy factories and the letter render legibly with the silhouettes intended (verified PNGs). I lowered the interlock actuator-core intensity (2.0→1.35) so the cowl-pop reveal reads as a real reveal rather than a pre-lit core. The gameplay contact sheet confirmed ring rhythm, blue→violet heat progression, wall/enemy readability, the charge whitening ramp, the full whiteout at the shot (t≈52.5s), and the star-streaked black-space muzzle exit.

**Perf/typecheck:** `npx tsc` — 0 errors, visuals grep empty. `npm run build` passes. `npm run check:perf` — all gates green after I fixed a geometry leak (the runner removes enemy/projectile meshes but doesn't free geometry; I made both `disposeRecord`s dispose the mesh — geometry count late dropped from 892→95, and to 26 post-shot).

**For the integrator to eyeball in a real WebGPU playtest:**
- The charge crescendo (bars ~24–28) is intentionally very bright and the shot is a ~0.6s full whiteout — confirm it reads as a launch and builds tension without fatigue at the player's bloom setting.
- The six-segment reticle charge-gauge legibility during live play (subtle at distance by design).
- The metallic gun-barrel camera rattle feel (tight, roll-biased, 12 Hz).
- Arc ball-lightning shell jitter uses per-frame `Math.random` (non-deterministic, visuals-only) — expected, but note it won't be identical between seeded snapshot runs.
